summary:
Claim: A removable prosthetic appliance used to cover palatal defects in cleft palate patients is best referred to as an obturator.  
Evidence: - Obsturators are routinely prescribed post-operatively to seal palatal gaps and improve speech and feeding outcomes. - Clinical guidelines recognize obturators as standard tools in managing residual palatal defects after surgical intervention.  
Answer: B
